Run-01 — B+ Configuration
Strain: B+ (P. cubensis)
Source: Inoculate the World · Batch 4685
Inoculated: Nov 28, 2025 · 4cc spore syringe
Grain: 1lb WBS pre-sterilized (1yr old at inoc)
Colonization: 101 days (tent · 68–74°F · dark · no air)
Transferred: March 9, 2026
Substrate: 3x Boomr Bag · loose mix · no compaction
Casing: ¼" Myco Coco coir · loose · not compacted
Tub: Green Tub · clear polyfill lid · steam pasteurized
Ratio: 3:1 substrate to grain (standard method)

Your Standard Grow Protocol

Phase 1 — Colonization

013–4cc spores injected into 1lb pre-sterilized grain bag via SAB
02Bag moves to small colonization tent · 68–74°F · dark · no moving air
03No break and shake · let mycelium network undisturbed
04Typical: 45–60 days · B+ Run-01 was 101 days (old bag)

Phase 2 — Bulk Transfer

013:1 ratio — 3x Boomr Bag substrate to 1x grain bag
02Break up grain bag and mix loosely with substrate — no compaction
03¼ inch loose Myco Coco coir casing layer on top — not compacted
04Clear polyfill filter patch lid · Bissell PowerSteamer to pasteurize environment
05IPA sterilization throughout · glove changes between stages

Phase 3 — Fruiting

01Move to fruiting tent · AC Infinity automated · 68–80°F avg 73°F
0270–90% RH · sunrise/sunset light cycle · external humidifier
03Typical: pins in 5–10 days · first flush in 2–3 weeks post-transfer
Grow Blog
Readable run journals · photo documentation · post-mortems
B+ — Run-01 · Active 🟡
Strain: B+ · P. cubensis
Source: ITW Batch 4685
Inoculated: Nov 28, 2025
Transferred: Mar 9, 2026
Tub: Green Tub

The Setup

B+ from Inoculate the World, Batch 4685. 4cc injected into a 1lb North Spore WBS pre-sterilized grain bag on November 28, 2025 — the same day as the APE run. The grain bag was approximately one year old at time of inoculation, which likely contributed to the extended colonization timeline.

Colonization — 101 Days

This run took 101 days in the colonization tent — well outside the typical 45–60 day window. Environment was consistent: 68–74°F, dark, no moving air, no break and shake. The mycelium eventually built a clean, robust network. The old grain bag is the working hypothesis for the extended timeline. Lesson: fresh grain bags matter.

Transfer to Bulk — March 9, 2026

Grain bag broken up and mixed loosely with 3x Boomr Bag manure-based substrate (3:1 ratio) in the Green Tub. No compaction — loose structure throughout to allow the mycelium to network freely. ¼ inch loose Myco Coco coir casing layer applied on top, not compacted. Clear polyfill filter patch lid installed. Environment steam pasteurized with Bissell PowerSteamer. IPA sterilization and glove changes throughout. Orange tub used as coco coir hydration vessel.

What's Next

FC initiated March 9. First pins estimated March 14–16. First flush estimated March 19–23. Based on your standard method and typical B+ performance at 68–74°F fruiting temps.

Yeti — Run-02 · Yeti-01 · Colonizing 🟡
Strain: Yeti · P. cubensis
Source: ITW Batch 4695
Inoculated: Mar 9, 2026
CC Used: 4cc · 6cc remaining
Bag ID: Yellow dot

Setup

Yeti from Inoculate the World, Batch 4695. 4cc injected into a fresh 1lb North Spore WBS pre-sterilized grain bag on March 9, 2026. Bag was approximately 1 year old at inoculation — same age range as the B+-01 bag that took 101 days. Watching closely to see if Yeti colonizes faster or if the bag age is again a variable.

Pre-Inoculation Protocol

Bag left at room temperature for 2 hours before inoculation, then shaken for 1 minute to loosen grain and distribute any settled moisture. This is a new prep step added to the protocol after the B+-01 experience — warmer grain and loosened kernels should give the mycelium better initial contact points.

Colonization

Yellow dot marker on bag for visual identification in the dark colonization tent. Into the tent at 68–74°F, dark, no moving air, no break and shake. Estimated colonization: 45–60 days standard window. Target transfer: late April to early May 2026.

Photo Journal — Inoculation Day · March 9, 2026
📷 Upload Yeti-01 inoculation photos
Tidal Wave (ITW) — Run-03 · TW-01 · Colonizing 🟡
Strain: Tidal Wave · P. cubensis
Source: ITW Batch 4660
Inoculated: Mar 9, 2026
CC Used: ~4cc est. · 6cc remaining
Bag ID: Blue dot

Setup

Tidal Wave from Inoculate the World, Batch 4660 — the ITW version, distinct from the older PNW Spore Co. Tidal Wave in inventory (flagged suspect, 3+ years old). This is a fresh 1yr syringe with a dense suspension. ~4cc estimated into bag on March 9, 2026.

Port Issue — Note for Log

The self-healing injection port clogged during inoculation. Some spore solution was lost — sprayed into the sink before flow was restored. Estimated ~4cc made it into the bag. 6cc remaining in syringe. Not enough to abort — watching colonization carefully. If growth is slow or patchy, the reduced inoculation volume is the likely cause.

Pre-Inoculation Protocol

Same as Yeti-01: room temperature 2 hours + 1 minute shake before injection. Blue dot marker on bag for visual ID in colonization tent.

Colonization

Into the tent at 68–74°F, dark, no moving air. Estimated 45–60 days if the reduced CC doesn't slow things down. This is also the first ITW Tidal Wave run — interesting comparison point against the PNW version that showed no potency.

Photo Journal — Inoculation Day · March 9, 2026
📷 Upload TW-01 inoculation photos
APE — Run Complete · Post-Mortem
Strain: Albino Penis Envy · P. cubensis
Source: ITW Batch 4696
Inoculated: Nov 28, 2025
F1 Harvest: ~76–82g dry
Ended: Mar 9, 2026 · Trichoderma F2

Setup & Environment

3cc APE spores into 1lb North Spore grain bag. Same colonization tent as B+. Pink shotgun tub with polyfill holes. Boomr Bag bulk substrate with Myco Coco coir casing. AC Infinity automated tent: avg 73°F, avg 75% RH, sunrise/sunset light cycle, external humidifier.

Colonization — 60 Days

APE colonized in 60 days — exactly within the standard window, and notably faster than the concurrent B+ run at 101 days despite identical conditions. No break and shake. Steady, even growth throughout.

First Flush — ~79g Dry

Clean, contamination-free run through flush one. Yield: ~76–82g dry / 2.6oz. Two ball jars packed. Harvest timing was dialed — veils just tearing. Dehydrated at 110°F for 24 hours, clusters split at 12-hour mark. Cracker dry result. Stored in Herb Guard vacuum jars at 68°F dark.

Second Flush — Trichoderma · Aborted

Three days into second flush, Trichoderma (green mold) detected. Moderate and spreading. Run aborted March 9, 2026 — contents dumped outdoors same morning the B+ tub was built. Pink tub cleaned, baking in Arizona sun today, will alcohol sterilize after. Tub status: Decon.

Lessons

Have desiccant packs on hand before harvest. Consider 5cc inoculation next APE run. Monitor Trichoderma risk more carefully at the between-flush transition — that window appears to be the vulnerability point in this environment.

Photo Journal — APE Run

Harvest Record
DateFlushDays FCWet(g)Dry(g)CountCap(cm)VeilColorBruiseSubstrateNotesPost-Harvest
—F1—————————F1 only. Trichoderma on F2 — run aborted Mar 9. Dehydrated 110°F 24hr, cracker dry. Processed into 0.25g caps via grain miller. Stored in Herb Guard vacuum jars, dark 68°F. Desiccant packs added. No side pin data recorded.—
—F1—————————Dense central flush, no side pinning. Minimal bruising pre-dryer, visible post-dryer. 4cc inoculation. Veils just breaking at harvest. Second flush pins visible. Bowl 1: 790g wet, Bowl 2: 682g wet. Dry weight pending. Dehydrating at 152°F Magic Mill. Second bowl held in fridge during first dryer run.—
